name: Volume Attached and Detached on Different Machines
notes: This rule triggers when a single volume is being attached and detached on multiple machines. In AWS environment as an example, replacing an SSH key pair involves switching volumes on different instances (the original and a temporary instance). Such manipulation of volumes could reveal a malicious behaviour.
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Volume Detached and Attached on the Same Machine
BB:BehaviorDefinition: Volume Detached and Attached on the Same Machine match at least 2 times with the same Volume ID (custom) and different Machine Identifier (custom) in 1 hour(s)
conditions_text: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Volume Detached and Attached on the Same Machine match at least 2 times with the same Volume ID (custom) and different Machine Identifier (custom) in 1 hour(s)
thresholds: when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Volume Detached and Attached on the Same Machine match at least 2 times with the same Volume ID (custom) and different Machine Identifier (custom) in 1 hour(s)
TD_list: APPLY Volume Attached and Detached on Different Machines on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when BB:BehaviorDefinition: Volume Detached and Attached on the Same Machine match at least 2 times with the same Volume ID (custom) and different Machine Identifier (custom) in 1 hour(s)
RED_list: Event name: Volume Attached and Detached on Different Machines
Event description: Detects a single volume that is attached and detached on multiple machines.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Volume ID (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
